records.ety: [F. installer, LL. installare, fr. pref. in- in + OHG. stal a place, stall, G. stall, akin to E. stall: cf. It. installare. See Stall.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Installed (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Installing.]

defs: To set in a seat; to give a place to; establish (one) in a place.
quotes: She installed her guest hospitably by the fireside.

defs: To place in an office, rank, or order; to invest with any charge by the usual ceremonies; to instate; to induct; as, to install an ordained minister as pastor of a church; to install a college president.
quotes: Unworthily Thou wast installed in that high degree.
